problem_text:
1. 某质点沿直线运动,位移 $ S $(单位:m)与时间 $ t $(单位:s)之间的关系为 $ S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 $,则当 $ t = 3 \, \text{s} $ 时该质点的瞬时速度为

A. 24 m/s 
B. 30 m/s 
C. 48 m/s 
D. 54 m/s

case_brief.problem_summary:
1. 某质点沿直线运动,位移 $ S $(单位:m)与时间 $ t $(单位:s)之间的关系为 $ S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 $,则当 $ t = 3 \, \text{s} $ 时该质点的瞬时速度为

A. 24 m/s 
B. 30 m/s 
C. 48 m/s 
D. 54 m/s
case_brief.student_state: student answer and process need diagnosis
observed_signals: Student process is incomplete for diagnostic checking.

missing_steps: Did not explicitly state that instantaneous velocity is the derivative of displacement
evidence_items: Student calculated 24 m/s but selected option B (30 m/s)

learning.primary_weakness: Calculus concept: derivative as instantaneous velocity
recommended_practice_focus: Relating displacement functions to velocity via derivatives
teacher_watchouts: Misclassification of calculus problem as geometry in curriculum
Students confusing displacement with velocity calculations

arbiter.problem_summary:
1. 某质点沿直线运动,位移 $ S $(单位:m)与时间 $ t $(单位:s)之间的关系为 $ S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 $,则当 $ t = 3 \, \text{s} $ 时该质点的瞬时速度为

A. 24 m/s 
B. 30 m/s 
C. 48 m/s 
D. 54 m/s
arbiter.primary_weakness: Calculus concept: derivative as instantaneous velocity
missing_steps: Did not explicitly state that instantaneous velocity is the derivative of displacement
evidence_items: Student calculated 24 m/s but selected option B (30 m/s)
recommended_practice_focus: Relating displacement functions to velocity via derivatives
arbiter.teacher_feedback_markdown: The student correctly computed the derivative $ S'(t) = 6t^2 - 10t $ and evaluated it at $ t=3 $ to get 24 m/s, but selected the incorrect option B (30 m/s). This indicates a possible answer selection error or conceptual confusion. Note: The problem is misclassified as 'geometry' in the curriculum when it actually requires calculus knowledge.

mastery_update_suggestion.note: Student demonstrated partial understanding of derivative calculation but failed to connect to correct answer selection. Requires reinforcement of calculus-kinematics relationship.

practice_focus: Relating displacement functions to velocity via derivatives
